zotero.abstractNote: We propose a method to differentiate through blackbox combinatorial solvers. Our approach allows for the integration of combinatorial algorithms (e.g., shortest path, max-flow, or even TSP solvers) into deep learning architectures as layers. The method is based on a local linear interpolation of the solver's solution surface, which is constructed using the solver's output at the forward pass and a perturbed version of the input at the backward pass. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several tasks, including image-based shortest path prediction and a challenging task of learning to solve the Traveling Salesperson Problem.
